rationale: Phase B Q01 search for exact-phrase 'Painlevé III hierarchy' returned 4 arXiv hits (Bilman-Ling-Miller 2018 arXiv:1806.00545, Bilman-Buckingham 2018 'Sakka PIII hierarchy', Atkin 2015 Lenard-recursion, mKdV 2024); web-search supplement added 2 more named higher-order-PIII papers (Atkin-Claeys-Mezzadri 2015 arXiv:1501.04475, Wang-Xu 2025 arXiv:2512.19381). 5 distinct PIII-hierarchy construction approaches identified in literature. Slot 181 HALT_E_LITERATURE_NULL signal does NOT hold under direct exact-phrase 'Painlevé III hierarchy' search (slot 181 used 18 keyword/author probes but not this phrase). The broad-scope structural-gap claim of slot 185 verdict ('no PIII hierarchy in the literature') is FALSIFIED.

impact.frontier_a_broad_scope: ABANDONED — broad PIII hierarchy scope falsified by 5 existing constructions
impact.frontier_a_narrow_scope: PLAUSIBLY_OPEN — specific Sakai-D6(1)-classification language (PIII(D6), Painlevé D6, Sakai D6) returns 0 arxiv hits; Frontier-A could re-scope to 'Sakai-geometric construction of PIII hierarchy' BUT requires (a) demonstrating existing 5 constructions are not Sakai-D6(1) surface-preserving in disguise + (b) re-framing contribution as alternative-construction not first-construction
impact.amendment_1_status: DOES_NOT_CLOSE_CLEANLY — 7/8 external identifiers PASS but entry 4 Joshi-Lustri-Topp 2014 RETRACTED as hallucinated; 2/10 SIARC-internal entries 9-10 not re-verified in this fire (deprioritized given HALT)
impact.amendment_2_status: CLEAR_BUT_MOOT — both entries 5+6 openly accessible on arxiv but Frontier-A re-scope may eliminate need for these refs
impact.amendment_3_status: CONFIRMED_BUT_MOOT — LR 2024 (arXiv:2407.03464) explicitly covers PIII3 = PIII(D6) degeneration
impact.amendment_4_status: MOOT — FIRE-A2 dual-witness MEDIUM ceiling moot until Frontier-A re-scopes

operator_decision_required: Re-vet Frontier-A scope via Claude (T1-Synth) consultation. Options: (A) abandon Frontier-A entirely and pursue different mathematical direction; (B) narrow to Sakai-D6(1)-geometric construction with mandatory pre-work to differentiate from 5 existing constructions; (C) reframe as 'survey + alternative-construction' rather than 'first PIII hierarchy'.

required_pre_rescope_reading_list.title: Random matrix ensembles with singularities and a hierarchy of Painlevé III equations
required_pre_rescope_reading_list.priority: HIGHEST — directly competes with Frontier-A's intended construction

required_pre_rescope_reading_list.title: Extreme Superposition: Rogue Waves of Infinite Order and the Painlevé-III Hierarchy
required_pre_rescope_reading_list.priority: HIGH — establishes the literature usage of 'Painlevé-III hierarchy' as a named object

required_pre_rescope_reading_list.title: Asymptotic and monodromy problems for higher-order Painlevé III equations
required_pre_rescope_reading_list.priority: HIGH — most recent (December 2025), directly named 'higher-order PIII equations'

required_pre_rescope_reading_list.title: Bäcklund Transformations for First and Second Painlevé Hierarchies
required_pre_rescope_reading_list.priority: MEDIUM — origin of 'Sakka PIII hierarchy' terminology (also see Sakka, J. Phys. A 42 (2009) 025210)

required_pre_rescope_reading_list.title: Painlevé III hierarchy via Lenard recursion (Atkin 2015 earlier paper)
